plainEnglish: At Level AAA, pre-recorded video with audio includes a sign-language interpretation of the audio. Many people who are deaf from birth use a sign language as their first language and read written text more slowly, so a signed interpretation (often shown picture-in-picture) gives them fuller access than captions alone.
whyItMatters: Deaf users whose first language is a sign language understand a signed interpretation far better than written captions, especially for complex content.
quickCheck: Check whether the video provides a sign-language interpreter track or a picture-in-picture signed version.
commonMistakes: Assuming captions remove the need for sign-language interpretation.
Showing the interpreter window so small or low-contrast that the signs are hard to read.
Cropping the interpreter so hands or facial expressions—which carry grammar—are cut off.

codeExamples.label: Main video with a synchronized sign-language video
Video with no sign-language interpretation
codeExamples.code:
<video controls src="/lecture.mp4">
  <track kind="captions" src="/lecture.en.vtt" srcLang="en" label="English" default />
</video>
